item.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree to ceasefire, officials say
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah have reached a Lebanon ceasefire agreement after their fighting delayed progression of the U.S.-Iran deal, a diplomat tells CBS News.

item.title: Vance postpones trip to Switzerland for U.S.-Iran negotiations as Israel strikes Lebanon
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is not headed to Switzerland, where U.S.-Iran talks were expected over the weekend. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Imtiaz Tyab explain why.

item.title: What's missing from the Epstein files? Here's what we found
item.description: The Justice Department says it's released "every document required by the Epstein Files Transparency Act," but CBS News has identified numerous gaps.

item.title: Italy nixes envoy's visit as leader Meloni "stunned" by Trump's remarks
item.description: Italian outlet says after a meeting at the G7 summit, Trump said Meloni had "begged me to take a picture with her," and he "felt sorry for her."

item.title: Latest news as Ebola cases rise in Africa, sparking global concerns
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in Africa continues to concern global health leaders as cases rise. CBS News' Ramy Inocencio reports from Uganda.

item.title: A look at U.S.-Australia World Cup match and surprises of tournament so far
item.description: Mike Grella, an analyst for CBS Sports Golazo Network, previews the United States' second match in the World Cup against Australia and breaks down the biggest surprises of the tournament so far.

item.title: Paint peeling in Reflecting Pool after multimillion-dollar makeover
item.description: Just two weeks after President Trump announced work at the Reflecting Pool in D.C. had been completed, paint is now peeling off. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Dangerous flooding in Louisiana and Mississippi as more severe weather looms
item.description: Dangerous flooding conditions in the Gulf states persist, and more extreme weather is expected. CBS News' Jason Allen reports.

item.title: More than a dozen dead after Israeli attack in Lebanon, potentially impacting peace deal
item.description: More than a dozen people in Lebanon are dead following an attack by Israel, officials said. Israel said it was retaliation for a deadly attack by Hezbollah. Imtiaz Tyab has the latest.

item.title: New book details the moment a GOP lawmaker appeared to lunge at Matt Gaetz
item.description: A new book recounts one of the most rambunctious moments on the floor of the House in modern history.

item.title: U.S.-Iran negotiations postponed as Netanyahu blasts Hezbollah over apparent attacks
item.description: Reported hostilities in Lebanon between Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group may have led to U.S.-Iran negotiations being postponed in Switzerland. CBS News' Taurean Small and the BBC's Hugo Bachega report, and Elisa Ewers, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, joins CBS News with more insight.

item.title: Record amount of sargassum seaweed found on Florida coast: "It stinks"
item.description: A record amount of sargassum seaweed has washed ashore on Florida beaches, bringing with it an unpleasant smell. Cristian Benavides shows how it's impacting coastal communities.

item.title: U.S. set for next World Cup match, but may be without star player
item.description: The U.S. men's soccer team will face Australia on Friday in their second match of the FIFA World Cup. They may be without star forward Christian Pulisic. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: Remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur flood homes and force evacuations in Gulf states
item.description: The remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur were battering parts of the southeastern U.S. with heavy rain, sparking flash flooding.

item.title: Tay Keith, Grammy-nominated producer, found dead in Nashville at 29
item.description: Record producer Tay Keith was found dead in his Nashville home by officers performing a welfare check, police said.

item.title: Obama Presidential Center dedicated with star-studded ceremony
item.description: The $850 million Obama Presidential Center in Chicago opens to the public on Friday. The former president led Thursday's dedication ceremony. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers withdraw psychiatric defense for state case in CEO killing
item.description: Luigi Mangione's lawyers are withdrawing a psychiatric defense for his New York state murder trial. A judge had previously revealed Mangione, who has pleaded not guilty, would claim extreme emotional disturbance at the time he allegedly shot and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. CBS News legal contributor Caroline Polisi explains.

item.title: Dozens of people rescued as dangerous flooding wreaks havoc on Gulf Coast
item.description: Severe flooding impacts millions of people along the Gulf Coast as rising waters inundated communities and prompted rescues. Jason Allen reports.

item.title: Mayor accused of faking her kidnapping in embezzlement scheme
item.description: Nancy Napoles denied the accusation and said she was willing to cooperate with authorities to clarify what happened.

item.title: Man charged in death of girl after life sentence commuted by Kristi Noem
item.description: McKenna Wendel was last seen alive on March 14 and her body was found outside Brookings, an hour's drive north of Sioux Falls, on March 19.

item.title: What's open and closed for Juneteenth 2026?
item.description: Juneteenth will affect banking, mail service and financial markets, although retailers and restaurants are largely staying open.

item.title: U.S.-Iran talks in Switzerland put on hold
item.description: The negotiations were expected to start as soon as this weekend, but Vice President Vance's trip there was put off and Switzerland said the negotiations have been postponed.

item.title: Cuba approves free-market reforms in effort to stave off economic collapse
item.description: Cuban lawmakers Thursday adopted nearly 200 historic free-market reforms aimed at rescuing the communist island from a severe crisis aggravated by a U.S. oil blockade.

item.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae turn water green
item.description: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's legal team withdraws psychiatric defense
item.description: Luigi Mangione's legal team says they withdrew a psychiatric defense​​ in his New York state murder trial one day after telling the court they would use it.

item.title: Gas prices fall below $4 a gallon for the first time in nearly 3 months
item.description: The national average for a gallon of regular gas in the U.S. drops to $3.99, the lowest since March 30.

item.title: Americans say they're reading fewer books. Here's what they're doing instead.
item.description: Many people are spending more time on screens, but also doing more physical activities, a new CBS News poll finds.

item.title: Supreme Court sides with man who challenged law barring drug users from having guns
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday ruled in favor of a Texas man who challenged a federal law that bars certain drug users from having firearms.

item.title: Trump formally signs U.S.-Iran deal as questions linger about nuclear program
item.description: President Trump warned he could order new strikes if Iran's leaders "don't behave." The U.S. and Iran signed the memo of understanding remotely, a White House official said.

item.title: Michigan plumbing company reunites woman with ring missing 10 years
item.description: Workers with Titan Plumbing in Howell, Michigan, were on an emergency call when they noticed what appeared to be an engagement ring. It turns out that the ring had been lost for more than a decade.
